mission.title: valkey: [NEW] Native windows support
mission.description: [NEW] Native windows support. Requested by 41+ users.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
**The problem/use-case that the feature addresses**

Make it run natively, without cygwin or wsl, on microsoft windows system.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [NEW] Native windows support
steps.description:
WiP to get build to work on Windows.

Might be best to have many small PRs than one big PR that takes F-O-R-E-V-E-R to merge. So I've named this branch `win32-build0`.
```yaml
BSD-3 licensed https://github.com/microsoftarchive/redis:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[NEW] Native windows support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
⚠️ Note: This resolution was work-in-progress at the time of PR submission. WiP to get build to work on Windows.

Might be best to have many small PRs than one big PR that takes F-O-R-E-V-E-R to merge. So I've named this branch `win32-build0`.
codeSnippets: BSD-3 licensed valkey, though really hasn't changed apart from renaming and `clang-format`ting since 7dae142a2ebf909a63df13e5813c073c79be521f:

Again, nothing non-straightforward to port to MSVC.

Finally let's look at:
